failure_modes.text: Analysis paralysis can delay action until the learning loop dies under bureaucracy and studies.
failure_modes.mitigation: Force a one-day answer: create a coherent summary and hypothesis at any point, then gather only enough data to test it.

failure_modes.text: Premature convergence can waste effort by boiling the ocean instead of gathering only enough facts to test the current hypothesis.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use the Pareto principle to identify the 20% of facts that drive 80% of the decision, and stop gathering beyond that threshold.

failure_modes.text: Agile theater can keep rituals running even while learning, accountability, and decision rights quietly disappear.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when agile rituals keep running but backlog churn replaces learning, accountability, or explicit decision rights.
failure_modes.mitigation: Pause between thought and action: in each retrospective, check whether the ritual produced a decision change, not just discussion.

premortem_questions.text: Is this the most important thing you could be doing right now?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is it the most important thing you could be doing right now?

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

heuristics.text: Run a standing one-day-answer synthesis so the team always knows its current hypothesis and next test.
Gather only enough facts to prove or disprove the current hypothesis instead of expanding discovery indefinitely.
heuristics.source_quote: gather only enough facts to prove or disprove a hypothesis

heuristics.text: Keep the goal-identify-diagnose-design-do loop sequential and continuous so action stays tied to diagnosis.
heuristics.source_quote: This loop must be performed sequentially and continuously for success.

curation_notes.summary: The chunk set stays close to the raw source's strongest intervention surface: one-day-answer discipline, anti-ocean-boiling focus, and explicit loop sequencing.
donor_drops: Did not preserve generic scrum or standup language because the raw file is much stronger on hypothesis and learning-loop discipline.
open_questions: The third heuristic compresses Dalio-style sequencing into an agile operating loop rather than preserving the original five-step phrasing verbatim.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on design thinking, systems thinking, and hypothesis-driven problem solving.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is rich on old-plan rigidity, hammer effects, and bureaucratic delay.
structured_tensions: The source explicitly names tensions against optimization-theory, premortem, and confirmation-bias.
